description:
You are acting as a configuration-manager who needs to track what has been *added* to a
        configuration file between two revisions.

        The following directory already exists:
        /home/user/configs

        Inside it there are two files that represent consecutive versions of the same
        configuration file:

        1. /home/user/configs/httpd.conf.prev   (the previous version)
        2. /home/user/configs/httpd.conf.curr   (the current version)

        Goal
        ----
        Create a new file called

            /home/user/configs/added_lines.log

        that contains **only** those complete lines that appear in
        “httpd.conf.curr” but are **absent** from “httpd.conf.prev”.

        Formatting rules for /home/user/configs/added_lines.log
        -------------------------------------------------------
        • Every line taken from the current file must be prefixed
          by the two-character string “+ ” (plus sign, followed by a single
          space).  
        • Preserve the original text of each added line exactly after the
          “+ “ prefix (no trimming or extra spaces).  
        • Keep the order in which the lines occur in
          “httpd.conf.curr”.  
        • Do **not** include any other symbols that a typical “diff” header
          would add (e.g. “@@”, “---”, “+++” etc.).  
        • Do **not** include lines that were removed or modified – only brand-new
          lines that never appeared in the previous version.  
        • Terminate the file with a single trailing newline character.

        Verification hint (what the grader will look for)
        --------------------------------------------------
        1. The file /home/user/configs/added_lines.log must exist.
        2. Its content must be byte-for-byte identical to the required format
           described above.
        3. There must be exactly two lines in the file, each beginning with “+ ”.

        You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line tool(s).  A single,
        well-composed shell command is sufficient to accomplish the task.
truth:
Initial state (already present when the agent starts)
        -----------------------------------------------------
        File: /home/user/configs/httpd.conf.prev
        -----------------------------------------------------
        # Apache HTTPD previous config
        ServerRoot "/etc/httpd"
        Listen 80
        Include conf.modules.d/*.conf
        User apache
        Group apache
        -----------------------------------------------------

        File: /home/user/configs/httpd.conf.curr
        -----------------------------------------------------
        # Apache HTTPD current config
        ServerRoot "/etc/httpd"
        Listen 80
        Listen 8080
        Include conf.modules.d/*.conf
        User apache
        Group apache
        -----------------------------------------------------

        Required final state for grading
        --------------------------------
        File: /home/user/configs/added_lines.log
        (must be created by the agent, permissions 0644 or more permissive)
        -----------------------------------------------------
        + # Apache HTTPD current config
        + Listen 8080
        -----------------------------------------------------
        (followed by exactly one newline at end of file)

        No other files need to be modified or created.
name: task_000000_c41977de
